company.name: krea.ai
company.notes: Frontier AI image/video generation. Surfaced 4 roles in 2026-05-20 LinkedIn scrape under AI Creative keywords. Direct creative-AI fit, Lior's stack overlap (Flux, etc.).

research.status_summary: Actively hiring across engineering, ML, and community roles as of June 2026, with no signals of layoffs, pauses, or acquisition.
research.hiring_policy: Krea operates full-time and in-person at its San Francisco waterfront office; remote appears limited to rare roles (e.g., Social Media Lead), and no public EOR or visa sponsorship policy has been stated — strong on-site SF bias makes EOR/remote unlikely.
recent_news: Krea 2 launched May 12–18, 2026 as the company's first proprietary foundation image model built from scratch, focused on aesthetics and creative control; went live for all users on May 18.

Krea 2 LoRA training previewed May 29, 2026 — lets creators train styles, characters, and objects privately or share publicly via Krea Train; API now available on Fal, ComfyUI, and with Claude/Codex support.

SeeDance-2 video generation tool announced April 16, 2026, adding an effects-tag catalog for controlling motion, style, lighting, and camera behavior; June 2, 2026 partnership with Henning Larsen Architects for AI architecture tools.
Krea Realtime 14B (14B-parameter real-time video model) released Oct 2025; FLUX.1 Krea open-sourced (weights + fine-tune resources) Jul 2025 — both still active pillars of current stack.
Krea 2 ranked #1 image model from an independent research lab and #6 globally on Artificial Analysis text-to-image leaderboard as of late May 2026.
research.growth_signal: Series B-stage company (~95 employees as of Apr 2026, up from earlier estimates) with $83M raised total (led by Bain Capital Ventures, a16z, Abstract Ventures), shipping proprietary foundation models at high cadence — clear upward product and headcount trajectory.
research.fit_for_lior: Near-perfect fit: Krea 2's LoRA/fine-tuning stack and FLUX.1 open release align directly with Lior's Flux expertise, and the rapid product surface expansion (nodes, realtime edit, moodboards, API) creates immediate openings for a Creative AI Product Analyst or Vibe Coder who can bridge generative model capabilities with UX and workflow design.
